hero_subtitle: SFTY connects sanctions alerts with wallet and transaction risk data to help compliance teams organize case workflows, preserve audit evidence, and streamline sanctions exposure review.

problem_title: Sanctions alerts create case management gaps
problem_description: Compliance teams face delays and manual effort when sanctions exposure alerts remain disconnected from transaction and wallet risk data. This separation complicates investigation workflows, weakens audit trails, and slows defensible case resolution.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates sanctions exposure alerts with wallet risk, transaction data and case management to streamline investigations, preserve audit evidence and support faster, defensible compliance reviews.
workflow_title: From sanctions exposure alerts to cases
workflow_description: SFTY routes sanctions exposure alerts through risk review, case creation, evidence collection and decision logging to support audit-ready compliance workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects sanctions alerts to cases, review workflows, decisions and audit-ready evidence, enabling compliance teams to manage sanctions exposure beyond isolated tools and alerts.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs managing sanctions exposure by connecting alert workflows with audit-ready evidence and governed deployment for clearer due diligence and operational control.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for sanctions case management?
faq_1_answer: SFTY structures sanctions exposure alerts into linked cases, connecting wallets, transactions, regulatory rules and provider data for clearer compliance workflows and investigative review.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle sanctions exposure alerts?
faq_2_answer: SFTY aggregates sanctions alerts from multiple providers, applies internal policies to prioritize and route them, then opens and links cases for efficient compliance team review.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, decision logs, alert details and supporting evidence in audit-ready formats to support internal reviews, bank due diligence and regulatory inspections.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with our compliance stack?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY sits above existing providers and compliance tools, connecting outputs into connected workflows without replacing your current stack or data sources.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace sanctions monitoring providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY complements sanctions monitoring providers by turning their outputs into structured workflows, cases, evidence and reports for better operational control.
final_title: Connect sanctions alerts to structured case workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY brings wallet risk, transaction alerts and sanctions exposure into audit-ready compliance cases and evidence.
